title: Nvidia donated a GB300 supercomputer to the Navy, to teach officers AI’s limits
excerpt: The Nvidia chip at the centre of America’s export fight with China has a new home: a classroom in Monterey. The Naval Postgraduate School has stood up a DGX GB300 AI supercomputer, the first in the US military, Nextgov reported. The machine runs on Nvidia’s Blackwell Ultra generation, the same line the US forbids selling […]
content_html:
The Nvidia chip at the centre of America’s export fight with China has a new home: a classroom in Monterey. The Naval Postgraduate School has stood up a DGX GB300 AI supercomputer, the first in the US military, Nextgov reported.

The machine runs on Nvidia’s Blackwell Ultra generation, the same line the US forbids selling to Chinese firms. This one Nvidia gave away, donating it to the school’s foundation. Under the hood sit 36 Grace CPUs and 72 Blackwell Ultra GPUs, wired to crunch work in parallel.

The school will use it to train students in weather and ocean modelling, operations research, cybersecurity, and resilience planning. It is not a data centre, the school said, but a single integrated system that slots into existing power and water. Firms including Vertiv, DDN, and VAST Data helped stand it up.

Nvidia framed the gift in patriotic terms, and it is also a showcase for its military-facing hardware. Chief executive Jensen Huang said the school’s people could “train on it, build with it, and lead America’s fighting force.”

Teaching the limits, not just the power

The more interesting part is the mission. The school does not want officers who simply trust the machine. “The question is not whether these technologies will exist,” said Captain Michael Owen, a Navy vice provost. It is “whether future military leaders understand them well enough to evaluate their limitations, apply them ethically and employ them responsibly.”

The supercomputer builds on a research pact between Nvidia and the school struck in December 2024. That deal already produced an AI Tech Center in 2025, kitted out with Nvidia’s Blackwell chips, Jetson devices, and its Omniverse simulation platform.

The symbolism is hard to miss. A chip too sensitive to sell to Beijing is now a teaching tool for American officers. The company that makes it handed it over for free. The lesson the Navy wants is not firepower, but judgement: knowing when the most advanced AI in the world should not be trusted.

Published July 23, 2026 - 2:41 pm UTC

Back to top

sentences.zh: 发布时间 UTC 时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 下午 2:41

content_html_zh:
处于美国对华出口战核心的英伟达芯片有了新家：蒙特雷的一间教室。 据 Nextgov 报道，海军研究生院已经推出了美国军方第一台 DGX GB300 人工智能超级计算机。

该机器运行的是 Nvidia Blackwell Ultra 系列，美国禁止向中国公司出售该系列产品。 英伟达将其赠送给了学校基金会。 引擎盖下有 36 个 Grace CPU 和 72 个 Blackwell Ultra GPU，用于并行处理工作。

学校将用它来培训学生天气和海洋建模、运筹学、网络安全和复原力规划。 学校表示，它不是一个数据中心，而是一个插入现有电力和水源的单一集成系统。 Vertiv、DDN 和 VAST Data 等公司帮助实现了这一目标。

英伟达以爱国的措辞表达了这份礼物，同时也是其面向军事的硬件的展示。 首席执行官黄仁勋表示，学校的人员可以“以此为基础进行训练，利用它进行建设，并领导美国的战斗力量。”

教导极限，而不仅仅是力量

更有趣的部分是任务。 学校不想要仅仅信任机器的官员。 “问题不在于这些技术是否会存在，”海军副教务长迈克尔·欧文上尉说。 问题在于“未来的军事领导人是否足够了解它们，以评估它们的局限性、合乎道德地应用它们并负责任地使用它们。”

这台超级计算机建立在 Nvidia 与学校于 2024 年 12 月达成的研究协议的基础上。 该交易已经在 2025 年建立了一个人工智能技术中心，配备了 Nvidia 的 Blackwell 芯片、Jetson 设备及其 Omniverse 模拟平台。

它的象征意义不容忽视。 一种过于敏感而无法出售给北京的芯片现在已成为美国军官的教学工具。 制造它的公司免费交付了它。 海军想要的教训不是火力，而是判断力：知道什么时候世界上最先进的人工智能不应该被信任。

发布时间 UTC 时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 下午 2:41

Back to top
